step-import-basic.title: Import a STEP File
step-import-basic.description: Load a STEP file (AP203/AP214 format) from your computer or cloud storage.
steps: Click 'Import STEP' button in the toolbar
Select a .step or .stp file
Module automatically routes to best parser (WASM, server, or cache)
Model loads in 3D viewport with automatic fit-to-view
tips: Small files (<30MB) parse instantly in browser
Large files (≥50MB) automatically use server converter
Imported models are cached for faster re-import

import-from-url.title: Import from URL
import-from-url.description: Load a STEP file directly from a URL (cloud storage, HTTP server, etc).
steps: Use API: cycleCAD.step.importFromURL('https://...')
Or drag STEP file from cloud provider to viewport
Supports Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive public links
File is downloaded and imported as if local

file-size-handling.title: Understanding File Size Categories
file-size-handling.description: cycleCAD automatically selects the best import strategy based on file size.

routing-strategy.title: How Import Routing Works
routing-strategy.description: The module intelligently selects parser based on file size, server availability, and browser capabilities.
algorithm: 2. If <30MB and Worker ready → use occt-import-js
3. Else if 30-50MB and OpenCascade ready → use OpenCascade.js
4. Else if server healthy → upload and parse server-side
6. If all fail → show error with recovery suggestions
tips: Routing is automatic—no user input needed
Worker heartbeat monitored for timeouts (90s limit)
Cache bypasses all parsing—instant load if file unchanged

server-setup.title: Setup Server Converter
server-setup.description: Deploy the FastAPI server for handling large STEP files server-side.
steps: Install Docker (if not already)
Pull image: docker pull cyclecad/converter:latest
Run container on port 8787
In cycleCAD, set Server URL to http://localhost:8787/convert
Import large files—they'll automatically route to server
cd ~/cyclecad/server
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn converter:app --port 8787

cloudDeploy.description: Deploy to cloud (AWS, Google Cloud, DigitalOcean, etc)

server-endpoints.title: Server API Endpoints
server-endpoints.description: The converter server exposes 3 main endpoints for STEP processing.

caching-strategy.title: GLB Caching System
caching-strategy.description: Imported models are cached in IndexedDB for instant re-import.
how-it-works: 1. File uploaded—module computes SHA-256 hash of first 1MB
5. Miss: Parse file and save GLB to cache
6. Automatic invalidation if file changed (hash mismatch)
benefits: No server load for cached files

adaptive-deflection.title: Adaptive Mesh Deflection
adaptive-deflection.description: Module automatically adjusts mesh density (deflection) based on file size.
customization: Affects: triangle count, file size, parsing time, visual quality
Smaller deflection = more triangles = slower but more accurate
Larger deflection = fewer triangles = faster but less detail

troubleshooting-memory.title: Troubleshooting: Memory Errors
troubleshooting-memory.problem: WASM parse failed: memory limit exceeded
causes: File too large for available browser memory (>100MB)
Complex geometry with millions of triangles
solutions: 1. Close other browser tabs to free memory
2. Use server converter instead (no browser memory limit)
3. Split assembly into smaller sub-assemblies in CAD
4. Reduce geometry detail before export
5. Increase browser memory allocation (varies by browser)
preventive: Always check file size before importing
Optimize STEP files in CAD before export

troubleshooting-timeout.title: Troubleshooting: Timeout Errors
troubleshooting-timeout.problem: WASM parsing timeout (90s). File too complex.
causes: File has >3000 parts or very complex geometry
solutions: 1. Use server converter (higher timeout: 5 minutes)
2. Split assembly and import parts separately
3. Open file in desktop CAD and verify it's valid
4. Reduce deflection to create coarser mesh faster
5. Check browser console for WASM errors
debugging: Check timeout: 90s for WASM, 300s for server

troubleshooting-server.title: Troubleshooting: Server Connection
solutions: 1. Check server running: curl http://localhost:8787/health
2. Verify URL in app settings: http://localhost:8787/convert
3. Check firewall: ensure port 8787 is open
4. Restart server: docker restart cyclecad-converter
5. Check server logs: docker logs cyclecad-converter
fallback: If server unavailable, app falls back to browser WASM
Large files will show error if server down

troubleshooting-geometry.title: Troubleshooting: Invalid or Deformed Geometry
troubleshooting-geometry.problem: Imported geometry is invisible, deformed, or incomplete
causes: Scale mismatch (STEP in meters, cycleCAD in mm)
Bad triangulation from deflection too coarse
solutions: 1. Check scale: open in Fusion/SolidWorks to verify dimensions
2. Toggle wireframe mode to see if mesh exists
3. Rescale geometry: multiply all coords by conversion factor
4. Re-import with smaller deflection (0.01 instead of 0.05)
5. Re-save file in original CAD software
6. Try online converter: https://products.aspose.app/cad/conversion
colorHandling: STEP file colors stored in geometry attributes
If not transferred: manually apply materials in cycleCAD
Check if original CAD has color info before export

export-step.title: Export to STEP Format
export-step.description: Save your cycleCAD model as a STEP file for use in other CAD software.
requirements: B-Rep kernel module must be loaded (optional for large models)
steps: 1. Click 'Export STEP' button (in STEP module panel)
2. Choose filename and location
3. File saved as AP214 STEP format
4. Open in Fusion 360, SolidWorks, FreeCAD, etc
limitations: Constraints and features not transferred (geometry only)
Colors may not transfer fully (depends on CAD software)
Some advanced geometry may be approximated
tips: B-Rep kernel provides highest fidelity export
Exported file can be re-imported for round-trip testing
Use for sharing designs or archival

programmatic-import.title: Programmatic STEP Import (API)
programmatic-import.description: Use JavaScript API to import STEP files in custom workflows.

basicUsage.description: Import a file from file input

best-practices.title: STEP Import Best Practices
optimization: Optimize file before export: remove unused features
Merge small cosmetic parts into sub-assemblies
Reduce NURBS surface complexity in CAD
Test file in desktop CAD before importing
performance: Set appropriate deflection for your use case
Import large files (>50MB) during non-peak hours
workflow: Import base model, then add details in cycleCAD
Use separate STEP files for sub-assemblies
Validate geometry after import (no invisible/deformed parts)
Export and re-import to test round-trip fidelity
production: Use server converter for production workflows
Monitor server health and uptime (5 nines recommended)
Configure caching and CDN for scalability
Log all imports for audit trail

step-versions.title: Supported STEP Versions
step-versions.description: cycleCAD supports AP203 and AP214 (most common). AP242 partially supported.

ap242.name: AP242 (2014)
ap242.notes: Geometry OK, color/metadata may be incomplete

conversion: FreeCAD: Open file, File > Export As > STEP
